sourceMovie.title: The Craft
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Craft for the same reason it broke through in 1996: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Andrew Fleming keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Needful Things
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Craft who respond to its horror will find Needful Things (1993) immediately familiar. Both films share a drama sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: A Dark Song
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Craft fans keep landing on A Dark Song (2016) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Warlock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Warlock draws from the same horror well as The Craft, channeling fantasy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1989 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Tales from the Darkside: The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Tales from the Darkside: The Movie (1990) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that The Craft later expanded. If The Craft resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Witch Hunt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror appeal of The Craft — that dark edge, the fantasy — translates cleanly into Witch Hunt (2021). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2 (2011) clearly understood what makes fantasy work — the same quality that keeps The Craft rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince (2009) is the kind of film The Craft fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The fantasy overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Mean Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mean Girls shares The Craft's gift for drama — the 2004 film delivers comedy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Hocus Pocus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Craft compelling — the fantasy, the comedy — shows up in Hocus Pocus (1993) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Fear Street: 1994
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of The Craft is alive in Fear Street: 1994 (2021), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Craft?
faq.answer: Try Needful Things, A Dark Song, Warlock—each shares DNA with The Craft while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Craft?
faq.answer: The Craft (1996) is best described as Horror, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Craft part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Craft Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
